title: Aspidium cuspidatopinnatum
taxonomy: Aspidium cuspidatopinnatum is classified as a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Aspidium, which is now considered to be a part of the broader genus Dryopteris. intro:
There is no information available on the Aspidium cuspidatopinnatum plant. However, Aspidium is an old genus of ferns, many of which have been reclassified under the genera Dryopteris and Matteuccia. 

A fern is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the division Pteridophyta. They are characterized by their leafy fronds that reproduce via spores.

The health benefits of ferns, including Aspidium species, are largely anecdotal and based on traditional use, but they are believed to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. 

Dryopteris species, which includes some plants previously classified as Aspidium, contain bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes.

The most common herbal preparations made from ferns, including Dryopteris species, are teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to support digestive health and reduce inflammation.
